messages.content: You output JSON for task plans.

You are a senior project planner producing structured task plans.

Follow this JSON schema:
TaskPlan { originalPrompt: string; subtasks: Subtask[]; totalSubtasks: number; decompositionStrategy?: string }
Subtask { id: string (kebab-case), title: string, description: string (>=10 chars), status: one of pending|in_progress|completed|failed, dependencies: string[], estimatedComplexity: low|medium|high, successCriteria: string }
Constraints: 2-10 subtasks, actionable descriptions, include logical dependencies, avoid circular references, IDs unique.
Original prompt: Framework: FastAPI, Styling: Tailwind CSS

Original request: create hello world app, where i can modify the text "hello world" colors, through a frontend ui
Clarifications:
- framework: FastAPI
- styling: Tailwind CSS
Previous validation issues:
None
Respond ONLY with JSON matching the schema.

response:
{
  "originalPrompt": "Framework: FastAPI, Styling: Tailwind CSS\n\nOriginal request: create hello world app, where i can modify the text \"hello world\" colors, through a frontend ui\nClarifications:\n- framework: FastAPI\n- styling: Tailwind CSS\nPrevious validation issues:\nNone",
  "decompositionStrategy": "Break the work into environment/setup, backend API, frontend UI with Tailwind, integration, and optional persistence. Start by initializing the project and installing dependencies so both backend and frontend steps can proceed in parallel where possible. Then configure Tailwind, implement the FastAPI endpoints, build the UI components, integrate them, and finally add persistence and basic tests.",
  "subtasks": [
    {
      "id": "init-fastapi-project",
      "title": "Initialize project repository and structure",
      "description": "Create a new project directory, initialize a git repo, create a virtual environment, and scaffold folders for backend (app/) and frontend (static/ or src/). Add basic README and .gitignore.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [],
      "estimatedComplexity": "low",
      "successCriteria": "Repository exists with virtualenv, app/ and static/ (or src/) folders, README and .gitignore present; git init completed without errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "install-dependencies",
      "title": "Install Python and frontend dependencies",
      "description": "Install FastAPI and Uvicorn in the virtual environment; add development tools (python-dotenv, pydantic optional). Install Node.js dependencies for Tailwind (postcss, autoprefixer, tailwindcss) and a small bundler (Vite or simple npm scripts).",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "init-fastapi-project"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "low",
      "successCriteria": "fastapi and uvicorn import successfully in Python REPL; npm install completes and tailwind CLI or build tool binaries are available."
    },
    {
      "id": "configure-tailwind",
      "title": "Configure Tailwind CSS for the frontend",
      "description": "Initialize Tailwind config, create a minimal input CSS file with Tailwind directives, and set up a build script to generate a final CSS file placed in the static/ folder served by FastAPI.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "install-dependencies"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "medium",
      "successCriteria": "tailwind.config.js created; running the build script outputs a CSS file with Tailwind utilities into the project static directory and the file includes expected utility classes."
    },
    {
      "id": "create-fastapi-endpoints",
      "title": "Implement FastAPI endpoints for getting/updating display settings",
      "description": "Create a minimal FastAPI app that serves static files and exposes JSON endpoints: GET /api/display to return current text and color, and POST /api/display to update the color and text. Include CORS if frontend served separately.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "install-dependencies"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "medium",
      "successCriteria": "FastAPI app runs with uvicorn, GET /api/display returns JSON with text and color fields, POST /api/display accepts JSON and returns the updated settings."
    },
    {
      "id": "build-frontend-ui",
      "title": "Create frontend UI using Tailwind to edit 'hello world' colors",
      "description": "Build an HTML page with Tailwind-styled controls: a color picker (or preset color buttons), input to edit text, and a preview that updates live. Place the HTML/CSS/JS in the static/ or src/ folder so it can be served by FastAPI.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "configure-tailwind"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "medium",
      "successCriteria": "Frontend displays 'hello world' text, has controls for color and text that update the preview instantly in the browser using local JS, and uses Tailwind classes for styling."
    },
    {
      "id": "integrate-frontend-and-api",
      "title": "Connect the frontend UI to the FastAPI endpoints",
      "description": "Implement JavaScript fetch calls from the UI to GET current settings on load and POST updates when the user changes the color or text. Ensure the UI updates after a successful POST and handle basic error states.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "create-fastapi-endpoints",
        "build-frontend-ui"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "high",
      "successCriteria": "On page load the UI fetches settings from GET /api/display; when user changes color or text the frontend POSTs to /api/display and the preview and subsequent reload reflect persisted changes or server response."
    },
    {
      "id": "add-persistence",
      "title": "Add simple persistence for display settings",
      "description": "Implement a lightweight persistence layer (file-based JSON or SQLite) so POSTed settings are saved server-side and survive restarts. Add basic validation and error handling on the backend.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "integrate-frontend-and-api"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "medium",
      "successCriteria": "After updating settings via UI and restarting the server, GET /api/display returns the last saved settings; errors are handled and invalid input is rejected with 4xx responses."
    }
  ],
  "totalSubtasks": 7
}
